articles.headline: India Unveils Green Hydrogen Mission Phase 2, Targets Global Leadership in Production and Export
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved Phase 2 of India's National Green Hydrogen Mission, significantly enhancing its scope and financial outlay.
• Key objectives include scaling up green hydrogen production capacity to 15 million metric tonnes per annum by 2030 and establishing India as a major exporter.
• The new phase emphasizes advanced electrolyser manufacturing, R&D in storage and transport technologies, and developing green hydrogen hubs.
• Financial incentives, including production-linked incentives (PLI) for electrolysers and green hydrogen, have been expanded.
• This initiative aims to decarbonize hard-to-abate sectors like refining, fertilizer, and steel, while also creating green jobs.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Economic Development
Environmental Pollution & Degradation
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Phase 2 of National Green Hydrogen Mission approved.
Target: 15 MTPA green hydrogen production by 2030.
Focus on R&D, manufacturing, and export.
PLI scheme for electrolysers and green hydrogen expanded.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's strategy to become a global leader in green hydrogen production and its implications for energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the role of financial incentives and technological advancements in achieving the mission's ambitious targets.
Examine the potential economic benefits, including job creation and industrial growth, and the challenges in infrastructure development and market creation.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen plant with electrolysers and renewable energy sources.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all normal matter.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, Emphasizes State's Duty to Protect Privacy
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, largely upheld the constitutional validity of several provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The court affirmed the state's legitimate interest in regulating digital data while reiterating the fundamental right to privacy.
• It directed the government to establish the Data Protection Board within the stipulated timeframe and ensure its independence.
• Concerns raised regarding the broad exemptions for government agencies were partially addressed, with the court suggesting stricter oversight mechanisms.
• The ruling provides clarity on data fiduciary responsibilities and data principal rights, setting a precedent for India's digital economy.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court upheld DPDP Act, 2023.
Affirmed fundamental right to privacy.
Directed establishment of independent Data Protection Board.
Addressed concerns on government exemptions, suggesting stricter oversight.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on India's data protection framework and its balance between state interests and individual privacy rights.
Discuss the challenges in implementing the DPDP Act effectively, particularly concerning the independence of the Data Protection Board and addressing government exemptions.
Evaluate the impact of this judgment on businesses, digital innovation, and citizens' trust in the digital ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: Gavel and law books symbolizing a court judgment, with digital data streams in the background.
articles.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the right to privacy a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Free Trade Agreement Review Concludes, Paving Way for Enhanced Economic Integration
articles.explainer:
• India and the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) successfully concluded the review of their Free Trade Agreement (FTA) in goods.
• The review aims to make the ASEAN-India Trade in Goods Agreement (AITIGA) more user-friendly, simple, and trade-facilitating.
• Key outcomes include tariff rationalization, streamlined customs procedures, and addressing non-tariff barriers to boost bilateral trade.
• The updated agreement is expected to significantly increase trade volume and investment flows between India and the ASEAN bloc.
• This move strengthens India's 'Act East' policy and its economic engagement with the Indo-Pacific region.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India-ASEAN FTA (AITIGA) review concluded.
Aims for user-friendly, simple, trade-facilitating agreement.
Focus on tariff rationalization and non-tariff barriers.
Strengthens India's 'Act East' policy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the updated India-ASEAN FTA in goods for India's economic diplomacy and its 'Act East' policy.
Discuss how the reviewed agreement can address existing challenges and unlock new opportunities for trade and investment between India and Southeast Asian nations.
Evaluate the potential impact on various sectors of the Indian economy and its role in regional supply chains.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states, symbolizing cooperation.
articles.trivia: ASEAN was established on 8 August 1967 in Bangkok, Thailand, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration).

articles.headline: New National Mangrove Protection and Restoration Plan Launched to Safeguard Coastal Ecosystems
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched a comprehensive 'National Mangrove Protection and Restoration Plan' with a significant financial outlay.
• The plan focuses on increasing India's mangrove cover, which acts as a natural barrier against cyclones and tsunamis, and a vital carbon sink.
• Key strategies include community participation, scientific research for species restoration, and integration with existing coastal zone management initiatives.
• Special emphasis is placed on states with vulnerable coastlines and degraded mangrove areas, such as West Bengal, Gujarat, and Andaman & Nicobar Islands.
• This initiative is critical for biodiversity conservation, climate change mitigation, and supporting coastal livelihoods.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Environment launched National Mangrove Protection and Restoration Plan.
Aims to increase mangrove cover, act as natural barrier and carbon sink.
Focus on community participation, scientific research.
Targets vulnerable coastal states like West Bengal, Gujarat.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and economic significance of mangroves for India's coastal regions and the challenges faced in their conservation.
Analyze the key features of the new National Mangrove Protection and Restoration Plan and its potential effectiveness in achieving conservation goals.
Examine the role of community involvement and scientific approaches in successful mangrove restoration and climate change adaptation.
articles.imageDescription: Aerial view of dense mangrove forests along a coastline.
articles.trivia: The Sundarbans, shared by India and Bangladesh, is the world's largest single block of tidal halophytic mangrove forest.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle Demonstrator, Paving Way for Cost-Effective Space Access
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone with the successful autonomous landing and recovery test of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) demonstrator.
• This mission, conducted at the Aeronautical Test Range, marks a crucial step towards developing a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) fully reusable launch system.
• The RLV technology aims to drastically reduce the cost of space launches, making satellite deployment and future human spaceflight missions more economically viable.
• Key technologies validated include hypersonic flight, autonomous navigation, and precision landing capabilities.
• This success positions India among a select few nations developing advanced reusable space technologies.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested RLV demonstrator for autonomous landing and recovery.
Aims to reduce space launch costs.
Validates hypersonic flight, autonomous navigation, precision landing.
Crucial for future human spaceflight and satellite deployment.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of ISRO's reusable launch vehicle technology for India's space program and its aspirations in the global space economy.
Discuss the technological challenges involved in developing fully reusable launch systems and how ISRO is addressing them.
Evaluate the potential impact of cost-effective space access on various sectors, including communication, earth observation, and scientific research.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle landing on a runway.
articles.trivia: The first successful orbital flight of a reusable spacecraft was by the Space Shuttle Columbia in 1981.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Signals Cautious Optimism on Inflation Trajectory Amidst Global Uncertainties
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• This decision reflects a balance between controlling inflation, which remains within the target band but with upward risks, and supporting economic growth.
• RBI projected India's GDP growth for FY27 at 7.2%, citing robust domestic demand and government capital expenditure.
• The MPC's stance remains 'withdrawal of accommodation,' indicating a readiness to act if inflation pressures resurface.
• Global geopolitical tensions and commodity price volatility were highlighted as key risks to the inflation outlook.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: RBI MPC kept repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
Stance: 'withdrawal of accommodation'.
Projected FY27 GDP growth at 7.2%.
Inflation within target band but with upward risks.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its implications for inflation management and economic growth in India.
Discuss the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance and how it balances the objectives of price stability and financial stability.
Examine the domestic and global factors influencing India's inflation trajectory and the challenges faced by the RBI in its monetary policy formulation.
articles.imageDescription: RBI logo and a graph showing stable interest rates.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Standing Committee Recommends Sweeping Reforms for Election Commission's Expenditure Monitoring
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Law and Justice submitted a report recommending significant reforms to enhance the Election Commission of India's (ECI) powers in monitoring election expenditure.
• The committee suggested bringing political parties' internal elections and funding under greater scrutiny to ensure transparency and accountability.
• Recommendations include granting ECI more statutory powers to enforce expenditure limits and penalize violations effectively.
• The report also highlighted the need for a robust mechanism to track and regulate 'dark money' in elections, advocating for real-time disclosure of donations.
• These reforms aim to strengthen electoral democracy and level the playing field for all political parties.
gsSubTopics: Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Standing Committee recommended ECI reforms for expenditure monitoring.
Suggested scrutiny of political parties' internal elections and funding.
Advocated for greater ECI statutory powers for enforcement.
Highlighted need to regulate 'dark money' and real-time disclosure.
mainsPoints: Discuss the existing challenges in monitoring election expenditure in India and the role of the Election Commission of India in ensuring free and fair elections.
Analyze the key recommendations of the Parliamentary Standing Committee regarding electoral reforms and their potential impact on transparency and accountability in political funding.
Evaluate the feasibility and implications of granting more statutory powers to the ECI and regulating 'dark money' in the electoral process.
articles.imageDescription: Symbolic image of ballot box and parliament building.
articles.trivia: Article 324 of the Indian Constitution provides for an Election Commission to superintend, direct and control the preparation of electoral rolls and the conduct of elections.

articles.headline: India Hosts G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors Meeting, Focuses on Global Debt and Climate Finance
articles.explainer:
• India hosted the first G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting of the year, bringing together key global economic leaders.
• The agenda focused on pressing global economic challenges, including rising sovereign debt burdens in developing countries and strengthening multilateral development banks.
• Discussions also centered on accelerating climate finance, particularly for energy transition and adaptation measures in vulnerable economies.
• India reiterated its commitment to fostering inclusive and sustainable global economic growth and addressing the fragmentation of the global financial system.
• The meeting laid the groundwork for further discussions leading up to the G20 Leaders' Summit later in the year.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India hosted G20 FMCBG meeting.
Key topics: global debt, climate finance, strengthening MDBs.
India emphasized inclusive and sustainable growth.
Prepares for G20 Leaders' Summit.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's role and objectives in hosting the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors meeting, particularly concerning global economic stability and development.
Discuss the challenges posed by rising global sovereign debt and the role of international cooperation in addressing them, as highlighted in the G20 discussions.
Examine the importance of climate finance for developing economies and how the G20 can facilitate greater investment in energy transition and climate resilience.
articles.imageDescription: G20 logo prominently displayed at a conference venue.
articles.trivia: The G20 was formed in 1999 in response to the Asian financial crisis as a forum for finance ministers and central bank governors to discuss global economic and financial issues.

articles.headline: Government Fast-Tracks 'Border Village Development Program' in Critical Northern and Northeastern Regions
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs announced the fast-tracking of the 'Border Village Development Program' (BVDP) focusing on enhancing infrastructure and livelihood opportunities in villages along India's northern and northeastern borders.
• The program aims to curb outward migration from these remote areas and strengthen national security by ensuring a vibrant local population.
• Key initiatives include improved road connectivity, telecom services, access to education and healthcare, and promotion of local tourism and agro-based industries.
• A dedicated inter-ministerial task force has been formed to ensure coordinated implementation and timely completion of projects.
• This strategic move is seen as vital for integrated border management and addressing demographic challenges in sensitive regions.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: MHA fast-tracked 'Border Village Development Program' (BVDP).
Focus on northern and northeastern border villages.
Aims to curb outward migration, strengthen national security.
Includes infrastructure, education, healthcare, tourism, agro-industries.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of border village development for India's national security and integrated border management.
Analyze the objectives and key components of the 'Border Village Development Program' and its potential impact on addressing demographic challenges and enhancing local livelihoods.
Examine the challenges in implementing such programs in remote and sensitive border areas and suggest measures for effective coordination and community participation.
articles.imageDescription: Scenic view of a remote village in the Himalayas, with improved road infrastructure.
articles.trivia: The 'Vibrant Villages Programme' was launched in 2023 for comprehensive development of villages along the Northern Border.

articles.headline: National Skill Development Mission 2.0 Launched with Focus on AI, Robotics, and Green Skills
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship launched 'National Skill Development Mission 2.0', a revamped initiative aimed at equipping India's youth with future-ready skills.
• The mission places significant emphasis on emerging technologies like Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Robotics, Data Science, and sustainable 'green skills'.
• It integrates industry partnerships, apprenticeship programs, and digital learning platforms to ensure demand-driven skill development.
• The new phase also focuses on upskilling and reskilling the existing workforce to adapt to evolving industry requirements.
• This initiative is crucial for enhancing India's demographic dividend and ensuring employability in a rapidly changing global economy.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors.
prelimsPoints: National Skill Development Mission 2.0 launched.
Focus on AI, Robotics, Data Science, Green Skills.
Integrates industry partnerships, apprenticeships, digital learning.
Aims to upskill/reskill existing workforce.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of skill requirements in the 21st century and the importance of adapting India's skill development programs accordingly.
Analyze the key features and objectives of the National Skill Development Mission 2.0 and its potential to leverage India's demographic dividend.
Examine the challenges in implementing large-scale skill development initiatives, particularly in integrating emerging technologies and ensuring industry relevance.
articles.imageDescription: Young people engaged in learning coding and robotics in a modern classroom.
articles.trivia: The first National Skill Development Mission was launched in 2015 to create convergence across sectors and states in terms of skill training activities.

articles.headline: Report Warns of Accelerating Himalayan Glacier Melt, Urges Urgent Climate Action and Adaptation Strategies
articles.explainer:
• A new report by the Ministry of Earth Sciences highlights an alarming acceleration in the melting rate of Himalayan glaciers, posing severe threats to water security and livelihoods.
• The study projects significant downstream impacts, including increased flood risks in the short term and potential water scarcity for millions in the long term.
• It calls for immediate and intensified national and international climate action to reduce greenhouse gas emissions.
• The report also emphasizes the need for robust adaptation strategies, such as improved early warning systems for glacial lake outburst floods (GLOFs) and sustainable water management practices.
• This finding underscores the vulnerability of the Hindu Kush Himalayan region to climate change and its far-reaching consequences.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: Report by Ministry of Earth Sciences on accelerating Himalayan glacier melt.
Warns of flood risks and long-term water scarcity.
Calls for urgent climate action and adaptation strategies.
Emphasizes early warning systems for GLOFs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multifaceted impacts of accelerating Himalayan glacier melt on India's water resources, agriculture, and disaster management challenges.
Analyze the urgent need for both climate change mitigation (emission reduction) and adaptation strategies in the Hindu Kush Himalayan region.
Evaluate the role of international cooperation and regional collaboration in addressing transboundary implications of glacial melt and ensuring water security for downstream communities.
articles.imageDescription: Melting glacier in the Himalayas with a visible glacial lake.
articles.trivia: The Himalayan region is sometimes referred to as the 'Third Pole' due to its vast ice and snow reserves outside the Arctic and Antarctic.

articles.headline: PLI Scheme for Advanced Battery Manufacturing Attracts Major Investments, Boosts EV Ecosystem
articles.explainer:
• The Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Storage manufacturing has successfully attracted significant domestic and international investments.
• Several major players have committed to setting up Giga-factories in India, exceeding the initial targets for production capacity.
• This surge in investment is crucial for localizing the electric vehicle (EV) supply chain and reducing dependence on imports for critical battery components.
• The scheme aims to make India a global manufacturing hub for ACC batteries, fostering innovation and creating high-skilled jobs.
• This development is a cornerstone of India's commitment to clean mobility and achieving net-zero emissions targets.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: PLI scheme for ACC Battery Storage manufacturing attracted major investments.
Exceeded initial targets for production capacity.
Aims to localize EV supply chain, reduce import dependence.
Positions India as global manufacturing hub for ACC batteries.
mainsPoints: Analyze the effectiveness of the Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme in boosting domestic manufacturing and attracting investments in the Advanced Chemistry Cell battery sector.
Discuss the significance of localizing battery manufacturing for India's electric vehicle ecosystem and its broader energy transition goals.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India to become a global hub for ACC battery production, considering technological advancements and raw material sourcing.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of an electric car battery pack with 'Made in India' label.
articles.trivia: Lithium-ion batteries are currently the most common type of ACC used in EVs, but research is ongoing for next-generation chemistries like solid-state batteries.

articles.headline: High Court Strikes Down Local Government Order Restricting Public Assembly, Citing Violation of Free Speech
articles.explainer:
• The High Court of [State Name] struck down an order issued by a local municipal corporation that imposed blanket restrictions on public assemblies and protests in certain areas.
• The court ruled that the order amounted to an arbitrary and disproportionate restriction on the fundamental rights to freedom of speech and expression and to assemble peacefully, guaranteed under Article 19 of the Constitution.
• It emphasized that while reasonable restrictions can be imposed, they must be proportionate, necessary, and clearly defined, not a blanket ban.
• The judgment underscored the importance of public spaces for democratic discourse and holding public authorities accountable.
• This ruling reaffirms the judiciary's role in safeguarding fundamental rights against arbitrary executive actions.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
prelimsPoints: High Court struck down local government order restricting public assembly.
Cited violation of Article 19 (Freedom of Speech and Assembly).
Emphasized proportionate and defined restrictions, not blanket bans.
Reaffirmed judiciary's role in safeguarding fundamental rights.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional provisions guaranteeing freedom of speech and assembly in India and the permissible limits of reasonable restrictions.
Analyze the significance of the High Court's judgment in upholding fundamental rights against potential executive overreach by local government bodies.
Evaluate the role of public spaces in democratic societies and the importance of judicial review in maintaining the balance between public order and civil liberties.
articles.imageDescription: A blurred image of a public protest with clear focus on a courthouse building.
articles.trivia: The Supreme Court in Ramlila Maidan Incident case (2012) held that the right to assemble peacefully is a fundamental right but is subject to reasonable restrictions.

articles.headline: India Establishes New AI Research Center in Collaboration with Leading Global Universities to Boost Indigenous AI Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) announced the establishment of a state-of-the-art National Centre for Artificial Intelligence Research (NCAIR).
• NCAIR is a collaborative initiative involving top Indian institutes and leading global universities, aimed at fostering cutting-edge research in AI.
• Its mandate includes developing indigenous AI solutions for critical sectors like healthcare, agriculture, education, and defense.
• The center will also focus on ethical AI development, data privacy, and building a robust AI talent pipeline through specialized training programs.
• This move is part of India's broader strategy to become a significant player in the global AI landscape, promoting both innovation and responsible AI deployment.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
keyTerms: National Centre for Artificial Intelligence Research (NCAIR)
prelimsPoints: MeitY established National Centre for Artificial Intelligence Research (NCAIR).
Collaboration with Indian and global universities.
Focus on indigenous AI solutions for healthcare, agriculture, education, defense.
Emphasizes ethical AI and talent development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing indigenous AI capabilities for India's economic growth, national security, and social development.
Analyze the key objectives and functions of the new National Centre for Artificial Intelligence Research (NCAIR) and its role in fostering innovation and ethical AI.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in building a robust AI ecosystem in India, including talent acquisition, data governance, and international collaboration.
articles.imageDescription: Futuristic laboratory setting with researchers working on AI algorithms.
articles.trivia: The term 'Artificial Intelligence' was coined by John McCarthy in 1956.

articles.headline: India Pledges Additional Humanitarian Aid for Disaster Relief in Neighboring Country Following Severe Flooding
articles.explainer:
• India announced a substantial increase in humanitarian assistance to a neighboring country severely impacted by recent catastrophic flooding.
• The aid package includes emergency relief supplies, medical teams, engineering expertise, and financial assistance for reconstruction efforts.
• This gesture underscores India's commitment to its 'Neighbourhood First' policy and its role as a first responder in regional crises.
• The assistance will be channeled through established bilateral mechanisms and in coordination with international aid agencies.
• The initiative highlights India's growing capacity and willingness to extend support during humanitarian emergencies in its vicinity.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India pledged additional aid to a flood-affected neighboring country.
Aid includes relief supplies, medical teams, financial assistance.
Underscores 'Neighbourhood First' policy.
Coordinated with international aid agencies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's humanitarian assistance and disaster relief (HADR) efforts in strengthening its 'Neighbourhood First' policy and regional influence.
Analyze how disaster diplomacy contributes to building trust and fostering stronger bilateral relations with neighboring countries.
Examine the challenges in coordinating and delivering effective humanitarian aid in complex disaster situations and the role of India as a first responder.
articles.imageDescription: Indian rescue workers providing aid in a flood-affected area.
articles.trivia: India has actively participated in HADR operations globally, including 'Operation Maitri' for Nepal earthquake (2015) and 'Operation Rahat' for Yemen evacuation (2015).

articles.headline: UNESCO Recognizes [Specific Site Name] as New World Heritage Site for its Unique Cultural Landscape and Biodiversity
articles.explainer:
• UNESCO has officially inscribed the '[Specific Site Name]' in [State Name], India, on its list of World Heritage Sites.
• The site was recognized for its exceptional universal value, combining a unique cultural landscape shaped by indigenous communities over centuries with rich biodiversity.
• This recognition highlights the sustainable practices of local communities in preserving both their cultural heritage and the natural environment.
• The inscription is expected to boost cultural tourism, generate local employment, and attract international funding for conservation efforts.
• It further solidifies India's position as a custodian of global heritage and promotes awareness of its diverse cultural and natural treasures.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including waterbodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: UNESCO inscribed '[Specific Site Name]' in [State Name] as a new World Heritage Site.
Recognized for unique cultural landscape and rich biodiversity.
Highlights sustainable practices of local communities.
Expected to boost tourism and conservation funding.
mainsPoints: Discuss the criteria and significance of a site being designated as a UNESCO World Heritage Site, particularly for sites with both cultural and natural value.
Analyze the potential benefits and challenges associated with such international recognition for local communities and conservation efforts in India.
Examine the role of indigenous practices and sustainable development in preserving cultural landscapes and biodiversity, using the newly recognized site as an example.
articles.imageDescription: A panoramic view of the newly recognized heritage site, showing ancient structures amidst lush greenery.
articles.trivia: To be included on the World Heritage List, sites must be of outstanding universal value and meet at least one of ten selection criteria.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends National Data Governance Framework for Ethical AI and Data Economy
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Communications and Information Technology submitted a report advocating for a comprehensive National Data Governance Framework.
• The framework aims to standardize data collection, storage, sharing, and usage across government and private sectors, with a strong emphasis on data ethics and privacy.
• Recommendations include establishing a central authority for data governance, ensuring interoperability of government datasets, and promoting data anonymization.
• The report stresses the importance of leveraging public data for economic growth and social welfare while safeguarding individual rights and national security.
• This move is seen as crucial for India's burgeoning data economy and its aspirations in Artificial Intelligence and digital public infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Committee recommended National Data Governance Framework.
Aims to standardize data collection, storage, sharing, usage.
Focus on data ethics, privacy, interoperability, anonymization.
Crucial for India's data economy and AI aspirations.
mainsPoints: Discuss the necessity of a robust National Data Governance Framework in the context of India's growing digital economy and the rise of Artificial Intelligence.
Analyze the key recommendations of the Parliamentary Standing Committee, particularly concerning data ethics, privacy, and the role of a central authority.
Examine the challenges in implementing a unified data governance policy across diverse sectors and ensuring a balance between data utilization for public good and individual rights.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract representation of data flow and governance structure.
articles.trivia: India's National Data Governance Framework Policy (NDGFP) was first drafted in 2022 to enable access to anonymized public data.

articles.headline: India's Solar Energy Capacity Sees Record Growth, Surpassing Targets for Renewable Energy Transition
articles.explainer:
• A report by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) indicates that India's solar energy capacity has witnessed unprecedented growth in the last fiscal year.
• The country has surpassed its short-term targets for solar power deployment, significantly contributing to its overall renewable energy goals.
• This growth is attributed to favorable government policies, declining solar panel costs, and increased private sector investment.
• The expansion of solar capacity is crucial for reducing India's carbon footprint, enhancing energy security, and meeting its international climate commitments.
• Challenges remain in grid integration, storage solutions, and land acquisition, but the momentum indicates a strong push towards a green energy future.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: India's solar energy capacity showed record growth.
Surpassed short-term targets for solar deployment.
Attributed to government policies, declining costs, private investment.
Crucial for carbon footprint reduction and energy security.
mainsPoints: Discuss the factors driving the rapid growth of India's solar energy capacity and its significance for the country's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the challenges associated with integrating large-scale renewable energy into the national grid and the importance of advanced energy storage solutions.
Evaluate the role of government policies and private sector investment in accelerating India's transition to a renewable energy-dominated future.
articles.imageDescription: Vast solar power farm stretching to the horizon under a clear sky.
articles.trivia: India is a founding member of the International Solar Alliance (ISA), an intergovernmental organization with a primary objective to promote solar energy.

articles.headline: Second Indigenous Aircraft Carrier 'INS Vishal' Commences Sea Trials, Bolstering India's Naval Capabilities
articles.explainer:
• India's second indigenously built aircraft carrier, 'INS Vishal', commenced its maiden sea trials from the Cochin Shipyard Limited.
• This marks a significant milestone in India's quest for self-reliance in naval defense and projecting power across the Indian Ocean Region.
• INS Vishal, a larger and more advanced carrier than INS Vikrant, is designed to operate a wider range of aircraft, including advanced fighter jets and helicopters.
• The carrier incorporates cutting-edge indigenous technologies, showcasing India's growing capabilities in complex warship construction.
• Its induction will further enhance the Indian Navy's blue-water capabilities and strategic reach, solidifying its role as a net security provider in the region.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: India's second indigenous aircraft carrier, 'INS Vishal', commenced sea trials.
Built by Cochin Shipyard Limited.
Larger and more advanced than INS Vikrant.
Enhances Indian Navy's blue-water capabilities and self-reliance.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of India's indigenous aircraft carrier program for its naval power projection and maritime security interests in the Indian Ocean Region.
Discuss the role of 'Make in India' in defence and the technological capabilities showcased by the construction of INS Vishal.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in achieving complete self-reliance in complex warship manufacturing and maintaining a robust naval fleet.
articles.imageDescription: INS Vishal, a massive aircraft carrier, sailing during its sea trials.
articles.trivia: India is one of only a handful of nations capable of designing and building aircraft carriers.

highlights: India launches Green Hydrogen Mission Phase 2, targeting 15 MTPA production by 2030 and global export leadership.
Supreme Court upholds key provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, emphasizing privacy and state's regulatory role.
ISRO successfully tests next-gen Reusable Launch Vehicle demonstrator, aiming for cost-effective space access.
RBI maintains repo rate at 6.5%, signals cautious optimism on inflation amidst global uncertainties.
Government fast-tracks 'Border Village Development Program' in northern and northeastern regions for integrated security and development.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to India's National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. Phase 2 aims to achieve 15 million metric tonnes per annum (MTPA) of green hydrogen production by 2030.
2. The mission primarily focuses on import of electrolysers and R&D in carbon capture technologies.
3. Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for electrolysers and green hydrogen has been expanded under this mission.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. Phase 2 targets 15 MTPA green hydrogen production by 2030. Statement 2 is incorrect. The mission emphasizes advanced electrolyser manufacturing (indigenous) and R&D in storage and transport technologies, not primarily carbon capture. Statement 3 is correct. Financial incentives, including PLI for electrolysers and green hydrogen, have been expanded.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC), constituted in 2016, is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India. While RBI implements the policy, the decision is taken by the MPC.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023:
1. It recognizes the fundamental right to privacy.
2. It mandates the establishment of a Data Protection Board.
3. The Supreme Court has entirely struck down all government exemptions under the Act.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as the Act is based on the recognition of the fundamental right to privacy. Statement 2 is correct, as the Act provides for the establishment of a Data Protection Board. Statement 3 is incorrect; the Supreme Court partially addressed concerns regarding broad exemptions for government agencies, suggesting stricter oversight, but did not entirely strike them down.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'Neighbourhood First' policy is associated with India's foreign relations primarily with which of the following?
options: (c) SAARC member states and other immediate neighbors
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) SAARC member states and other immediate neighbors
prelimsMocks.explanation: India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy is a core tenet of its foreign policy, emphasizing friendly and cooperative relations with its immediate neighbors, including SAARC member states, with a focus on mutual growth and prosperity.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements best describes the primary objective of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program?
options: (a) To develop a new generation of intercontinental ballistic missiles.
(b) To reduce the cost of space launches and make space access more economical.
(c) To establish a permanent human outpost on the Moon by 2030.
(d) To develop advanced satellite communication systems for military use.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) To reduce the cost of space launches and make space access more economical.
prelimsMocks.explanation: ISRO's RLV program aims to develop fully reusable launch systems to drastically reduce the cost of space launches, making satellite deployment and future human spaceflight missions more economically viable. Options (a), (c), and (d) are not the primary objectives of the RLV program.

mainsMocks.question: India's pursuit of green hydrogen as a key energy source is a strategic imperative for both energy security and climate action. Discuss the potential of the National Green Hydrogen Mission to transform India's energy landscape and the challenges in its large-scale implementation. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Economic Development; Environmental Pollution & Degradation
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Green Hydrogen Mission and its significance. Discuss its potential to diversify energy mix, reduce imports, create jobs, and meet climate goals. Highlight challenges like high production costs, infrastructure development, R&D needs, and market creation. Conclude with policy support and international collaboration.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's pronouncements on data protection underscore the delicate balance between individual privacy rights and the state's legitimate interests in a digital age. Analyze the implications of the recent ruling on the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) for India's data governance framework. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—Fundamental Rights; Government policies and interventions; Statutory bodies
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by briefly explaining the DPDP Act and the fundamental right to privacy. Discuss how the SC ruling affirms this right while acknowledging the need for regulation. Analyze implications for data fiduciaries, data principals, and government agencies, particularly regarding exemptions. Discuss the role of the Data Protection Board and the need for robust oversight. Conclude on strengthening India's digital governance.

mainsMocks.question: Border area development is crucial for integrated national security and addressing demographic challenges in sensitive regions. In this context, critically examine the objectives and potential impact of the 'Border Village Development Program' (BVDP) in India. (10 marks, 150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Security challenges and their management in border areas; GS2: Government policies and interventions
mainsMocks.approach: Define the significance of border areas for national security. Explain the BVDP's objectives: curbing migration, improving infrastructure, livelihood opportunities. Discuss its potential impact on strengthening local economies, fostering loyalty, and enhancing surveillance. Critically assess challenges like remote terrain, resource allocation, and effective coordination. Conclude on its strategic importance.

quickBytes.text: India's National Green Hydrogen Mission Phase 2 aims for 15 MTPA production by 2030.
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023, provides for the establishment of a Data Protection Board.
ASEAN was founded in 1967 with the signing of the Bangkok Declaration.
Mangroves act as natural barriers against cyclones and tsunamis, and are vital carbon sinks.
ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program aims to reduce space launch costs.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets India's benchmark interest rate (repo rate).
Article 324 of the Indian Constitution empowers the Election Commission of India.
The G20 was formed in 1999 in response to the Asian financial crisis.
The 'Vibrant Villages Programme' focuses on comprehensive development of villages along India's northern border.
Lithium-ion batteries are the most common Advanced Chemistry Cells (ACC) used in Electric Vehicles (EVs).

GS1.headline: UNESCO Recognizes [Specific Site Name] as New World Heritage Site for its Unique Cultural Landscape and Biodiversity
GS1.explainer:
• UNESCO has officially inscribed the '[Specific Site Name]' in [State Name], India, on its list of World Heritage Sites.
• The site was recognized for its exceptional universal value, combining a unique cultural landscape shaped by indigenous communities over centuries with rich biodiversity.
• This recognition highlights the sustainable practices of local communities in preserving both their cultural heritage and the natural environment.
• The inscription is expected to boost cultural tourism, generate local employment, and attract international funding for conservation efforts.
• It further solidifies India's position as a custodian of global heritage and promotes awareness of its diverse cultural and natural treasures.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including waterbodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: UNESCO inscribed '[Specific Site Name]' in [State Name] as a new World Heritage Site.
Recognized for unique cultural landscape and rich biodiversity.
Highlights sustainable practices of local communities.
Expected to boost tourism and conservation funding.
mainsPoints: Discuss the criteria and significance of a site being designated as a UNESCO World Heritage Site, particularly for sites with both cultural and natural value.
Analyze the potential benefits and challenges associated with such international recognition for local communities and conservation efforts in India.
Examine the role of indigenous practices and sustainable development in preserving cultural landscapes and biodiversity, using the newly recognized site as an example.
GS1.imageDescription: A panoramic view of the newly recognized heritage site, showing ancient structures amidst lush greenery.
GS1.trivia: To be included on the World Heritage List, sites must be of outstanding universal value and meet at least one of ten selection criteria.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, Emphasizes State's Duty to Protect Privacy
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, largely upheld the constitutional validity of several provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The court affirmed the state's legitimate interest in regulating digital data while reiterating the fundamental right to privacy.
• It directed the government to establish the Data Protection Board within the stipulated timeframe and ensure its independence.
• Concerns raised regarding the broad exemptions for government agencies were partially addressed, with the court suggesting stricter oversight mechanisms.
• The ruling provides clarity on data fiduciary responsibilities and data principal rights, setting a precedent for India's digital economy.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court upheld DPDP Act, 2023.
Affirmed fundamental right to privacy.
Directed establishment of independent Data Protection Board.
Addressed concerns on government exemptions, suggesting stricter oversight.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on India's data protection framework and its balance between state interests and individual privacy rights.
Discuss the challenges in implementing the DPDP Act effectively, particularly concerning the independence of the Data Protection Board and addressing government exemptions.
Evaluate the impact of this judgment on businesses, digital innovation, and citizens' trust in the digital ecosystem.
GS2.imageDescription: Gavel and law books symbolizing a court judgment, with digital data streams in the background.
GS2.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the right to privacy a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Free Trade Agreement Review Concludes, Paving Way for Enhanced Economic Integration
GS2.explainer:
• India and the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) successfully concluded the review of their Free Trade Agreement (FTA) in goods.
• The review aims to make the ASEAN-India Trade in Goods Agreement (AITIGA) more user-friendly, simple, and trade-facilitating.
• Key outcomes include tariff rationalization, streamlined customs procedures, and addressing non-tariff barriers to boost bilateral trade.
• The updated agreement is expected to significantly increase trade volume and investment flows between India and the ASEAN bloc.
• This move strengthens India's 'Act East' policy and its economic engagement with the Indo-Pacific region.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India-ASEAN FTA (AITIGA) review concluded.
Aims for user-friendly, simple, trade-facilitating agreement.
Focus on tariff rationalization and non-tariff barriers.
Strengthens India's 'Act East' policy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the updated India-ASEAN FTA in goods for India's economic diplomacy and its 'Act East' policy.
Discuss how the reviewed agreement can address existing challenges and unlock new opportunities for trade and investment between India and Southeast Asian nations.
Evaluate the potential impact on various sectors of the Indian economy and its role in regional supply chains.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states, symbolizing cooperation.
GS2.trivia: ASEAN was established on 8 August 1967 in Bangkok, Thailand, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration).

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Standing Committee Recommends Sweeping Reforms for Election Commission's Expenditure Monitoring
GS2.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Law and Justice submitted a report recommending significant reforms to enhance the Election Commission of India's (ECI) powers in monitoring election expenditure.
• The committee suggested bringing political parties' internal elections and funding under greater scrutiny to ensure transparency and accountability.
• Recommendations include granting ECI more statutory powers to enforce expenditure limits and penalize violations effectively.
• The report also highlighted the need for a robust mechanism to track and regulate 'dark money' in elections, advocating for real-time disclosure of donations.
• These reforms aim to strengthen electoral democracy and level the playing field for all political parties.
gsSubTopics: Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Standing Committee recommended ECI reforms for expenditure monitoring.
Suggested scrutiny of political parties' internal elections and funding.
Advocated for greater ECI statutory powers for enforcement.
Highlighted need to regulate 'dark money' and real-time disclosure.
mainsPoints: Discuss the existing challenges in monitoring election expenditure in India and the role of the Election Commission of India in ensuring free and fair elections.
Analyze the key recommendations of the Parliamentary Standing Committee regarding electoral reforms and their potential impact on transparency and accountability in political funding.
Evaluate the feasibility and implications of granting more statutory powers to the ECI and regulating 'dark money' in the electoral process.
GS2.imageDescription: Symbolic image of ballot box and parliament building.
GS2.trivia: Article 324 of the Indian Constitution provides for an Election Commission to superintend, direct and control the preparation of electoral rolls and the conduct of elections.

GS2.headline: India Hosts G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors Meeting, Focuses on Global Debt and Climate Finance
GS2.explainer:
• India hosted the first G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting of the year, bringing together key global economic leaders.
• The agenda focused on pressing global economic challenges, including rising sovereign debt burdens in developing countries and strengthening multilateral development banks.
• Discussions also centered on accelerating climate finance, particularly for energy transition and adaptation measures in vulnerable economies.
• India reiterated its commitment to fostering inclusive and sustainable global economic growth and addressing the fragmentation of the global financial system.
• The meeting laid the groundwork for further discussions leading up to the G20 Leaders' Summit later in the year.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India hosted G20 FMCBG meeting.
Key topics: global debt, climate finance, strengthening MDBs.
India emphasized inclusive and sustainable growth.
Prepares for G20 Leaders' Summit.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's role and objectives in hosting the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors meeting, particularly concerning global economic stability and development.
Discuss the challenges posed by rising global sovereign debt and the role of international cooperation in addressing them, as highlighted in the G20 discussions.
Examine the importance of climate finance for developing economies and how the G20 can facilitate greater investment in energy transition and climate resilience.
GS2.imageDescription: G20 logo prominently displayed at a conference venue.
GS2.trivia: The G20 was formed in 1999 in response to the Asian financial crisis as a forum for finance ministers and central bank governors to discuss global economic and financial issues.

GS2.headline: National Skill Development Mission 2.0 Launched with Focus on AI, Robotics, and Green Skills
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship launched 'National Skill Development Mission 2.0', a revamped initiative aimed at equipping India's youth with future-ready skills.
• The mission places significant emphasis on emerging technologies like Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Robotics, Data Science, and sustainable 'green skills'.
• It integrates industry partnerships, apprenticeship programs, and digital learning platforms to ensure demand-driven skill development.
• The new phase also focuses on upskilling and reskilling the existing workforce to adapt to evolving industry requirements.
• This initiative is crucial for enhancing India's demographic dividend and ensuring employability in a rapidly changing global economy.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors.
prelimsPoints: National Skill Development Mission 2.0 launched.
Focus on AI, Robotics, Data Science, Green Skills.
Integrates industry partnerships, apprenticeships, digital learning.
Aims to upskill/reskill existing workforce.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of skill requirements in the 21st century and the importance of adapting India's skill development programs accordingly.
Analyze the key features and objectives of the National Skill Development Mission 2.0 and its potential to leverage India's demographic dividend.
Examine the challenges in implementing large-scale skill development initiatives, particularly in integrating emerging technologies and ensuring industry relevance.
GS2.imageDescription: Young people engaged in learning coding and robotics in a modern classroom.
GS2.trivia: The first National Skill Development Mission was launched in 2015 to create convergence across sectors and states in terms of skill training activities.

GS2.headline: High Court Strikes Down Local Government Order Restricting Public Assembly, Citing Violation of Free Speech
GS2.explainer:
• The High Court of [State Name] struck down an order issued by a local municipal corporation that imposed blanket restrictions on public assemblies and protests in certain areas.
• The court ruled that the order amounted to an arbitrary and disproportionate restriction on the fundamental rights to freedom of speech and expression and to assemble peacefully, guaranteed under Article 19 of the Constitution.
• It emphasized that while reasonable restrictions can be imposed, they must be proportionate, necessary, and clearly defined, not a blanket ban.
• The judgment underscored the importance of public spaces for democratic discourse and holding public authorities accountable.
• This ruling reaffirms the judiciary's role in safeguarding fundamental rights against arbitrary executive actions.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
prelimsPoints: High Court struck down local government order restricting public assembly.
Cited violation of Article 19 (Freedom of Speech and Assembly).
Emphasized proportionate and defined restrictions, not blanket bans.
Reaffirmed judiciary's role in safeguarding fundamental rights.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional provisions guaranteeing freedom of speech and assembly in India and the permissible limits of reasonable restrictions.
Analyze the significance of the High Court's judgment in upholding fundamental rights against potential executive overreach by local government bodies.
Evaluate the role of public spaces in democratic societies and the importance of judicial review in maintaining the balance between public order and civil liberties.
GS2.imageDescription: A blurred image of a public protest with clear focus on a courthouse building.
GS2.trivia: The Supreme Court in Ramlila Maidan Incident case (2012) held that the right to assemble peacefully is a fundamental right but is subject to reasonable restrictions.

GS2.headline: India Pledges Additional Humanitarian Aid for Disaster Relief in Neighboring Country Following Severe Flooding
GS2.explainer:
• India announced a substantial increase in humanitarian assistance to a neighboring country severely impacted by recent catastrophic flooding.
• The aid package includes emergency relief supplies, medical teams, engineering expertise, and financial assistance for reconstruction efforts.
• This gesture underscores India's commitment to its 'Neighbourhood First' policy and its role as a first responder in regional crises.
• The assistance will be channeled through established bilateral mechanisms and in coordination with international aid agencies.
• The initiative highlights India's growing capacity and willingness to extend support during humanitarian emergencies in its vicinity.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India pledged additional aid to a flood-affected neighboring country.
Aid includes relief supplies, medical teams, financial assistance.
Underscores 'Neighbourhood First' policy.
Coordinated with international aid agencies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's humanitarian assistance and disaster relief (HADR) efforts in strengthening its 'Neighbourhood First' policy and regional influence.
Analyze how disaster diplomacy contributes to building trust and fostering stronger bilateral relations with neighboring countries.
Examine the challenges in coordinating and delivering effective humanitarian aid in complex disaster situations and the role of India as a first responder.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian rescue workers providing aid in a flood-affected area.
GS2.trivia: India has actively participated in HADR operations globally, including 'Operation Maitri' for Nepal earthquake (2015) and 'Operation Rahat' for Yemen evacuation (2015).

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends National Data Governance Framework for Ethical AI and Data Economy
GS2.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Communications and Information Technology submitted a report advocating for a comprehensive National Data Governance Framework.
• The framework aims to standardize data collection, storage, sharing, and usage across government and private sectors, with a strong emphasis on data ethics and privacy.
• Recommendations include establishing a central authority for data governance, ensuring interoperability of government datasets, and promoting data anonymization.
• The report stresses the importance of leveraging public data for economic growth and social welfare while safeguarding individual rights and national security.
• This move is seen as crucial for India's burgeoning data economy and its aspirations in Artificial Intelligence and digital public infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Committee recommended National Data Governance Framework.
Aims to standardize data collection, storage, sharing, usage.
Focus on data ethics, privacy, interoperability, anonymization.
Crucial for India's data economy and AI aspirations.
mainsPoints: Discuss the necessity of a robust National Data Governance Framework in the context of India's growing digital economy and the rise of Artificial Intelligence.
Analyze the key recommendations of the Parliamentary Standing Committee, particularly concerning data ethics, privacy, and the role of a central authority.
Examine the challenges in implementing a unified data governance policy across diverse sectors and ensuring a balance between data utilization for public good and individual rights.
GS2.imageDescription: Abstract representation of data flow and governance structure.
GS2.trivia: India's National Data Governance Framework Policy (NDGFP) was first drafted in 2022 to enable access to anonymized public data.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Green Hydrogen Mission Phase 2, Targets Global Leadership in Production and Export
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved Phase 2 of India's National Green Hydrogen Mission, significantly enhancing its scope and financial outlay.
• Key objectives include scaling up green hydrogen production capacity to 15 million metric tonnes per annum by 2030 and establishing India as a major exporter.
• The new phase emphasizes advanced electrolyser manufacturing, R&D in storage and transport technologies, and developing green hydrogen hubs.
• Financial incentives, including production-linked incentives (PLI) for electrolysers and green hydrogen, have been expanded.
• This initiative aims to decarbonize hard-to-abate sectors like refining, fertilizer, and steel, while also creating green jobs.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Economic Development
Environmental Pollution & Degradation
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Phase 2 of National Green Hydrogen Mission approved.
Target: 15 MTPA green hydrogen production by 2030.
Focus on R&D, manufacturing, and export.
PLI scheme for electrolysers and green hydrogen expanded.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's strategy to become a global leader in green hydrogen production and its implications for energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the role of financial incentives and technological advancements in achieving the mission's ambitious targets.
Examine the potential economic benefits, including job creation and industrial growth, and the challenges in infrastructure development and market creation.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen plant with electrolysers and renewable energy sources.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all normal matter.

GS3.headline: New National Mangrove Protection and Restoration Plan Launched to Safeguard Coastal Ecosystems
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched a comprehensive 'National Mangrove Protection and Restoration Plan' with a significant financial outlay.
• The plan focuses on increasing India's mangrove cover, which acts as a natural barrier against cyclones and tsunamis, and a vital carbon sink.
• Key strategies include community participation, scientific research for species restoration, and integration with existing coastal zone management initiatives.
• Special emphasis is placed on states with vulnerable coastlines and degraded mangrove areas, such as West Bengal, Gujarat, and Andaman & Nicobar Islands.
• This initiative is critical for biodiversity conservation, climate change mitigation, and supporting coastal livelihoods.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Environment launched National Mangrove Protection and Restoration Plan.
Aims to increase mangrove cover, act as natural barrier and carbon sink.
Focus on community participation, scientific research.
Targets vulnerable coastal states like West Bengal, Gujarat.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and economic significance of mangroves for India's coastal regions and the challenges faced in their conservation.
Analyze the key features of the new National Mangrove Protection and Restoration Plan and its potential effectiveness in achieving conservation goals.
Examine the role of community involvement and scientific approaches in successful mangrove restoration and climate change adaptation.
GS3.imageDescription: Aerial view of dense mangrove forests along a coastline.
GS3.trivia: The Sundarbans, shared by India and Bangladesh, is the world's largest single block of tidal halophytic mangrove forest.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle Demonstrator, Paving Way for Cost-Effective Space Access
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone with the successful autonomous landing and recovery test of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) demonstrator.
• This mission, conducted at the Aeronautical Test Range, marks a crucial step towards developing a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) fully reusable launch system.
• The RLV technology aims to drastically reduce the cost of space launches, making satellite deployment and future human spaceflight missions more economically viable.
• Key technologies validated include hypersonic flight, autonomous navigation, and precision landing capabilities.
• This success positions India among a select few nations developing advanced reusable space technologies.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested RLV demonstrator for autonomous landing and recovery.
Aims to reduce space launch costs.
Validates hypersonic flight, autonomous navigation, precision landing.
Crucial for future human spaceflight and satellite deployment.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of ISRO's reusable launch vehicle technology for India's space program and its aspirations in the global space economy.
Discuss the technological challenges involved in developing fully reusable launch systems and how ISRO is addressing them.
Evaluate the potential impact of cost-effective space access on various sectors, including communication, earth observation, and scientific research.
GS3.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle landing on a runway.
GS3.trivia: The first successful orbital flight of a reusable spacecraft was by the Space Shuttle Columbia in 1981.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Signals Cautious Optimism on Inflation Trajectory Amidst Global Uncertainties
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• This decision reflects a balance between controlling inflation, which remains within the target band but with upward risks, and supporting economic growth.
• RBI projected India's GDP growth for FY27 at 7.2%, citing robust domestic demand and government capital expenditure.
• The MPC's stance remains 'withdrawal of accommodation,' indicating a readiness to act if inflation pressures resurface.
• Global geopolitical tensions and commodity price volatility were highlighted as key risks to the inflation outlook.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: RBI MPC kept repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
Stance: 'withdrawal of accommodation'.
Projected FY27 GDP growth at 7.2%.
Inflation within target band but with upward risks.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its implications for inflation management and economic growth in India.
Discuss the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance and how it balances the objectives of price stability and financial stability.
Examine the domestic and global factors influencing India's inflation trajectory and the challenges faced by the RBI in its monetary policy formulation.
GS3.imageDescription: RBI logo and a graph showing stable interest rates.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.

GS3.headline: Government Fast-Tracks 'Border Village Development Program' in Critical Northern and Northeastern Regions
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs announced the fast-tracking of the 'Border Village Development Program' (BVDP) focusing on enhancing infrastructure and livelihood opportunities in villages along India's northern and northeastern borders.
• The program aims to curb outward migration from these remote areas and strengthen national security by ensuring a vibrant local population.
• Key initiatives include improved road connectivity, telecom services, access to education and healthcare, and promotion of local tourism and agro-based industries.
• A dedicated inter-ministerial task force has been formed to ensure coordinated implementation and timely completion of projects.
• This strategic move is seen as vital for integrated border management and addressing demographic challenges in sensitive regions.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: MHA fast-tracked 'Border Village Development Program' (BVDP).
Focus on northern and northeastern border villages.
Aims to curb outward migration, strengthen national security.
Includes infrastructure, education, healthcare, tourism, agro-industries.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of border village development for India's national security and integrated border management.
Analyze the objectives and key components of the 'Border Village Development Program' and its potential impact on addressing demographic challenges and enhancing local livelihoods.
Examine the challenges in implementing such programs in remote and sensitive border areas and suggest measures for effective coordination and community participation.
GS3.imageDescription: Scenic view of a remote village in the Himalayas, with improved road infrastructure.
GS3.trivia: The 'Vibrant Villages Programme' was launched in 2023 for comprehensive development of villages along the Northern Border.

GS3.headline: Report Warns of Accelerating Himalayan Glacier Melt, Urges Urgent Climate Action and Adaptation Strategies
GS3.explainer:
• A new report by the Ministry of Earth Sciences highlights an alarming acceleration in the melting rate of Himalayan glaciers, posing severe threats to water security and livelihoods.
• The study projects significant downstream impacts, including increased flood risks in the short term and potential water scarcity for millions in the long term.
• It calls for immediate and intensified national and international climate action to reduce greenhouse gas emissions.
• The report also emphasizes the need for robust adaptation strategies, such as improved early warning systems for glacial lake outburst floods (GLOFs) and sustainable water management practices.
• This finding underscores the vulnerability of the Hindu Kush Himalayan region to climate change and its far-reaching consequences.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: Report by Ministry of Earth Sciences on accelerating Himalayan glacier melt.
Warns of flood risks and long-term water scarcity.
Calls for urgent climate action and adaptation strategies.
Emphasizes early warning systems for GLOFs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multifaceted impacts of accelerating Himalayan glacier melt on India's water resources, agriculture, and disaster management challenges.
Analyze the urgent need for both climate change mitigation (emission reduction) and adaptation strategies in the Hindu Kush Himalayan region.
Evaluate the role of international cooperation and regional collaboration in addressing transboundary implications of glacial melt and ensuring water security for downstream communities.
GS3.imageDescription: Melting glacier in the Himalayas with a visible glacial lake.
GS3.trivia: The Himalayan region is sometimes referred to as the 'Third Pole' due to its vast ice and snow reserves outside the Arctic and Antarctic.

GS3.headline: PLI Scheme for Advanced Battery Manufacturing Attracts Major Investments, Boosts EV Ecosystem
GS3.explainer:
• The Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Storage manufacturing has successfully attracted significant domestic and international investments.
• Several major players have committed to setting up Giga-factories in India, exceeding the initial targets for production capacity.
• This surge in investment is crucial for localizing the electric vehicle (EV) supply chain and reducing dependence on imports for critical battery components.
• The scheme aims to make India a global manufacturing hub for ACC batteries, fostering innovation and creating high-skilled jobs.
• This development is a cornerstone of India's commitment to clean mobility and achieving net-zero emissions targets.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: PLI scheme for ACC Battery Storage manufacturing attracted major investments.
Exceeded initial targets for production capacity.
Aims to localize EV supply chain, reduce import dependence.
Positions India as global manufacturing hub for ACC batteries.
mainsPoints: Analyze the effectiveness of the Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme in boosting domestic manufacturing and attracting investments in the Advanced Chemistry Cell battery sector.
Discuss the significance of localizing battery manufacturing for India's electric vehicle ecosystem and its broader energy transition goals.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India to become a global hub for ACC battery production, considering technological advancements and raw material sourcing.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of an electric car battery pack with 'Made in India' label.
