GS3.trivia: Lithium-ion batteries are currently the most common type of ACC used in EVs, but research is ongoing for next-generation chemistries like solid-state batteries.

GS3.headline: India Establishes New AI Research Center in Collaboration with Leading Global Universities to Boost Indigenous AI Development
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) announced the establishment of a state-of-the-art National Centre for Artificial Intelligence Research (NCAIR).
• NCAIR is a collaborative initiative involving top Indian institutes and leading global universities, aimed at fostering cutting-edge research in AI.
• Its mandate includes developing indigenous AI solutions for critical sectors like healthcare, agriculture, education, and defense.
• The center will also focus on ethical AI development, data privacy, and building a robust AI talent pipeline through specialized training programs.
• This move is part of India's broader strategy to become a significant player in the global AI landscape, promoting both innovation and responsible AI deployment.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
keyTerms: National Centre for Artificial Intelligence Research (NCAIR)
prelimsPoints: MeitY established National Centre for Artificial Intelligence Research (NCAIR).
Collaboration with Indian and global universities.
Focus on indigenous AI solutions for healthcare, agriculture, education, defense.
Emphasizes ethical AI and talent development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing indigenous AI capabilities for India's economic growth, national security, and social development.
Analyze the key objectives and functions of the new National Centre for Artificial Intelligence Research (NCAIR) and its role in fostering innovation and ethical AI.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in building a robust AI ecosystem in India, including talent acquisition, data governance, and international collaboration.
GS3.imageDescription: Futuristic laboratory setting with researchers working on AI algorithms.
GS3.trivia: The term 'Artificial Intelligence' was coined by John McCarthy in 1956.

GS3.headline: India's Solar Energy Capacity Sees Record Growth, Surpassing Targets for Renewable Energy Transition
GS3.explainer:
• A report by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) indicates that India's solar energy capacity has witnessed unprecedented growth in the last fiscal year.
• The country has surpassed its short-term targets for solar power deployment, significantly contributing to its overall renewable energy goals.
• This growth is attributed to favorable government policies, declining solar panel costs, and increased private sector investment.
• The expansion of solar capacity is crucial for reducing India's carbon footprint, enhancing energy security, and meeting its international climate commitments.
• Challenges remain in grid integration, storage solutions, and land acquisition, but the momentum indicates a strong push towards a green energy future.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: India's solar energy capacity showed record growth.
Surpassed short-term targets for solar deployment.
Attributed to government policies, declining costs, private investment.
Crucial for carbon footprint reduction and energy security.
mainsPoints: Discuss the factors driving the rapid growth of India's solar energy capacity and its significance for the country's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the challenges associated with integrating large-scale renewable energy into the national grid and the importance of advanced energy storage solutions.
Evaluate the role of government policies and private sector investment in accelerating India's transition to a renewable energy-dominated future.
GS3.imageDescription: Vast solar power farm stretching to the horizon under a clear sky.
GS3.trivia: India is a founding member of the International Solar Alliance (ISA), an intergovernmental organization with a primary objective to promote solar energy.

GS3.headline: Second Indigenous Aircraft Carrier 'INS Vishal' Commences Sea Trials, Bolstering India's Naval Capabilities
GS3.explainer:
• India's second indigenously built aircraft carrier, 'INS Vishal', commenced its maiden sea trials from the Cochin Shipyard Limited.
• This marks a significant milestone in India's quest for self-reliance in naval defense and projecting power across the Indian Ocean Region.
• INS Vishal, a larger and more advanced carrier than INS Vikrant, is designed to operate a wider range of aircraft, including advanced fighter jets and helicopters.
• The carrier incorporates cutting-edge indigenous technologies, showcasing India's growing capabilities in complex warship construction.
• Its induction will further enhance the Indian Navy's blue-water capabilities and strategic reach, solidifying its role as a net security provider in the region.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: India's second indigenous aircraft carrier, 'INS Vishal', commenced sea trials.
Built by Cochin Shipyard Limited.
Larger and more advanced than INS Vikrant.
Enhances Indian Navy's blue-water capabilities and self-reliance.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of India's indigenous aircraft carrier program for its naval power projection and maritime security interests in the Indian Ocean Region.
Discuss the role of 'Make in India' in defence and the technological capabilities showcased by the construction of INS Vishal.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in achieving complete self-reliance in complex warship manufacturing and maintaining a robust naval fleet.
GS3.imageDescription: INS Vishal, a massive aircraft carrier, sailing during its sea trials.
GS3.trivia: India is one of only a handful of nations capable of designing and building aircraft carriers.
